perturbation.original_text: Each of RevMed and Sanofi will use its commercially reasonable good faith efforts to remove promptly any and all impediments to consummation of the transaction contemplated by this Agreement, including obtaining government antitrust clearance, cooperating in good faith with any Governmental Authority investigation, promptly producing any documents and information and providing witness testimony if requested by a Governmental Authority.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of RevMed and Sanofi will use its good faith efforts to attempt to remove impediments to the transaction contemplated by this Agreement, including obtaining government antitrust clearance, cooperating with any Governmental Authority investigation, producing documents and information and providing witness testimony if requested by a Governmental Authority.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that both parties will 'use its commercially reasonable good faith efforts to remove promptly any and all impediments'. The modified text changes the requirement to 'use its good faith efforts to attempt to remove impediments'. The removal of 'commercially reasonable' introduces ambiguity, potentially allowing parties to exert less effort than would be required under a commercial standard. The word 'attempt' introduces uncertainty, since the outcome of 'attempting' is not guaranteed.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text potentially contradicts the Hart-Scott-Rodino Act because companies are legally obligated to comply with antitrust regulations, and simply 'attempting' to do so without commercially reasonable diligence could be seen as a failure to meet the legal standard of compliance.

Premerger notification and waiting period (a) Filing Except as exempted pursuant to subsection (c), no person shall acquire, directly or indirectly, any voting securities or assets of any other person, unless both persons (or in the case of a tender offer, the acquiring person) file notification pursuant to rules under subsection (d)(1) and the waiting period described in subse
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to remove impediments to the transaction. The original text requires 'commercially reasonable good faith efforts to remove promptly any and all impediments,' while the changed text requires 'good faith efforts to attempt to remove impediments.' The removal of 'commercially reasonable' and the addition of 'attempt' introduce ambiguity and uncertainty. The Law Explanation correctly identifies that companies are obligated to comply with antitrust regulations, and the scraped law snippet 1, 15 USC 18a, discusses premerger notification requirements, which fall under antitrust regulations. The change from 'remove promptly' to 'attempt to remove' weakens the commitment and introduces ambiguity in the level of effort required, potentially contradicting the legal obligation to comply with antitrust regulations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall perform all Research activities under this Agreement in compliance with all Applicable Law (including GMP, GLP and GCP). In furtherance and not in limitation of the foregoing, RevMed shall use diligent efforts to conduct its activities under each Research Plan in accordance with the terms of such Research Plan (including timelines), as the same may be amended from time to time (and which basis for comparison shall be tolled until any then-contemplated or pending amendments are completed or for the duration of any bona fide dispute between the Parties with respect to a Research Plan or amendment thereto), and this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall endeavor to perform all Research activities under this Agreement in general accordance with Applicable Law. RevMed will try to use diligent efforts to conduct its activities under each Research Plan, bearing in mind the terms of such Research Plan.
perturbation.explanation: The original text has clear obligations: 'shall perform...in compliance with all Applicable Law' and 'RevMed shall use diligent efforts'. The modified text weakens these obligations. 'Endeavor to perform...in general accordance' allows for deviations from strict legal compliance. 'Will try to use diligent efforts, bearing in mind the terms' makes the efforts discretionary and dependent on subjective interpretation of the Research Plan's terms.
perturbation.law_explanation: This change contradicts GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) regulations (21 CFR Part 58), which mandate strict adherence to defined protocols and quality standards in non-clinical laboratory studies. The modification suggests a more flexible approach, creating ambiguity as to whether the research activities will consistently meet the necessary standards required by law.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates strict compliance with Applicable Law, including GMP, GLP, and GCP. The modified text weakens this obligation to 'endeavor to perform...in general accordance with Applicable Law'. This creates an ambiguity regarding whether the research activities will consistently meet the necessary standards required by law, as explained in the LLM generated Law Explanation. Snippet 1, referencing 21 CFR Part 58, explicitly states it covers 'Good Laboratory Practice for Nonclinical Laboratory Studies,' implying a strict regulatory framework. The modified language introduces ambiguity that could lead to non-compliance with this framework, thus creating a contradiction as defined in the Ambiguities Legal Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Sanofi will reimburse RevMed for RevMed R&D Costs incurred by or on behalf of RevMed after the Execution Date in the performance of its activities under the Development Plan, as applicable, provided that such RevMed R&D Costs are incurred per the Development Budget, as applicable, for such activities as approved by the JSC and do not exceed [***]% of the applicable amounts set forth in the Development Budget for the particular Calendar Quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: Sanofi may reimburse RevMed for RevMed R&D Costs incurred by or on behalf of RevMed after the Execution Date in the performance of its activities under the Development Plan, as applicable, considering if such RevMed R&D Costs are generally aligned with the Development Budget, as applicable, for such activities as informally discussed by the JSC.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that 'Sanofi will reimburse RevMed,' setting a firm obligation. The changed text replaces 'will reimburse' with 'may reimburse,' making reimbursement discretionary. Furthermore, the original requires that the R&D costs are 'per the Development Budget', while the modified text replaces it with 'generally aligned with the Development Budget, as applicable, for such activities as informally discussed by the JSC'. This introduces further ambiguity by changing a previously set requirement with a less specific recommendation.
perturbation.law_explanation: This change introduces a potential conflict with 2 CFR § 200.403, which outlines principles for determining costs applicable to federal awards. While this contract isn't explicitly a federal award, the principle of cost allowability, reasonableness, and allocability is generally expected in contracts. The change introduces ambiguity into the cost reimbursement process, potentially conflicting with requirements for clear and consistent cost accounting practices.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 2 CFR § 200.403 - Factors affecting allowability of costs. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 2 CFR § 200.403 - Factors affecting allowability of costs. CFR prev | next § 200.403 Factors affecting allowability of costs. Except where otherwise authorized by statute, costs must meet the following criteria to be allowable under Federal awards: (a) Be necessary and reasonable for the performance of the Federal award and be allocable thereto under these principles. (b) Conform to any limitations or exclusions set forth in these principles or in the Federal award as to types or amount of cost items. (c) Be consistent with policies and procedures that apply uniformly to both federally financed and other activities of the recipient or subrecipient. (d) Be accorded consistent treatment. For example, a cost must not be assigned to a Federal award as a direct cost if any other cost incurred for the same purpose in like circumstances has been allocated to the Federal award as an indirect cost. (e) Be determined in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP), except, for State and local governments and Indian Tribes only, as otherwise provided for in this part. (f) Not be included as a cost or used to meet cost sharing requirements of any other federally-financed program in either the current or a prior period. See § 200.306(b) . (g) Be adequately documented. See §§ 200.300 through 200.309. (h) Administrative closeout costs may be incurred until the due date of the final report(s). If incurred, these costs must be liquidated prior to the due date of the final report(s) and charged to the final budget period of the award unless otherwise specified by the Federal agency. All other costs must be incurred during the approved budget period. At its discretion, the Federal agency is authorized to waive prior written approvals to carry forward unobligated balances to subsequen
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the principle of cost allowability by making reimbursement discretionary instead of mandatory. The original text stated 'Sanofi will reimburse RevMed,' while the changed text uses 'Sanofi may reimburse RevMed.' 2 CFR § 200.403 outlines factors affecting the allowability of costs under Federal awards. While this contract is not explicitly a federal award, the principle of cost allowability is weakened by the change from 'will' to 'may,' introducing ambiguity. The change from strict adherence to the Development Budget to alignment with informal JSC discussions further weakens cost control, potentially conflicting with consistent cost accounting practices implied within 2 CFR § 200.403. Additionally, the original text required that the R&D costs are 'per the Development Budget', while the modified text replaces it with 'generally aligned with the Development Budget, as applicable, for such activities as informally discussed by the JSC', making it difficult to enforce.
